content.problemStatement:
This problem has no story, so we at least wish you good luck solving it.

Consider a multiset SSS which contains AAA copies of the integer 111, BBB copies of 222 and CCC copies of 333. For example, if A=3A = 3A=3, B=3B = 3B=3 and C=3C = 3C=3, then S={1,1,1,2,2,2,3,3,3}S = \{1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3\}S={1,1,1,2,2,2,3,3,3}.

Your goal is to divide the elements of SSS into two groups (multisets); each element of SSS must be in exactly one of these groups and the sum of elements of one group must be equal to the sum of elements of the other group. In our example, it is possible to make the groups {1,1,2,2,3}\{1, 1, 2, 2, 3\}{1,1,2,2,3} and {1,2,3,3}\{1, 2, 3, 3\}{1,2,3,3}; the sum of elements of each group is 999.

Determine if it is possible to make such groups.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers AAA, BBB and CCC.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if it is possible to create two groups with equal sums or `"NO"` if it is impossible.
